title: helianthemum punctatum
intro: Helianthemum punctatum, also known as prairie fire or spotted rockrose, is a perennial plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum punctatum can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
